title: rubus subcarpinifolius
intro: Rubus subcarpinifolius, also known as the dwarf raspberry or thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to East Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The leaves, stems, and roots of the Rubus subcarpinifolius plant can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to alleviate various health issues. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive problems.
